english: used with reference to possession (penes), mostly as partic. or adj., and construed like bcás-pa (bcas pa), having, being possessed of, provided with, = càn (can) (which in W. is almost exclusively used in this sense). The objects may be things of any description, also physical and mental properties, so that ldán-pa (ldan pa) differs in this respect from bcás-pa (bcas pa) (Tar. 136, 14. 15); nor daṅ ldán-pa (nor dang ldan pa) rich, wealthy; sems-can daṅ ldán-pa (sems can dang ldan pa) with child; bu daṅ bu-mor - ldán - pa (bu dang bu mor ldan pa) having children; rig-pa daṅ ldán-pa (rig pa dang ldan pa) wise; with a negative: nor daṅ mi ldán-pa (nor dang mi ldan pa); daṅ ldan-par gyúr-ba (dang ldan par gyur ba) to get, to obtain, frq.; ldán-du lén-pa (ldan du len pa) Glr. 101, 1 is stated to mean the same. Poetically, and forming part of certain expressions and names, without daṅ (dang) and pa (pa), like càn (can): nor-ldán (nor ldan), dga-ldán (dga' ldan), byor-ldán ('byor ldan). — 3. ldán-pa (ldan pa) and ldan daṅ dis-pa (ldan dang dis pa) seem to imply: mixed, compound (opp. to rkyaṅ-pa (rkyang pa)) with regard to temper and disposition of mind S. g. — 4. to add up, sum up, Wdk. — 5. W. *gún-ka tsúg-pa ldan yin* (*gun ka tsug pa ldan yin*) it will be enough, it will hold out, till winter - time, prob. only a corruption of ldáṅ-ba (ldang ba). — 6. Pur. = grig (grig), regularly, properly, duly, rightly.
one that has, that is able, a man of ability Cs.
n. of a country Ma.
equivalent to °dus-tsád (°dus tshad) Mng. 35.(?).
Lex. silly talk, tittle-tattle.
Lex. w.e., Cs. indolence, dullness, drowsiness; acc. to others, a hasty, volatile manner.

pf. bldabs (bldabs), fut. bldab (bldab), imp. ldob (ldob), 1. Cs. to do again, to repeat; skyár-ldáb (skyar ldab) Lex., Sch.: repeatedly, anew, afresh, again; ñis-ldáb (nyis ldab) Lex., Sch.: for the second time, doubly, twice; *čú-(l)dab de saṅ čéṅ-mo yod* (*cu (l)dab de sang cheng mo yod*) W. it is ten times as large as that, yet cf. ltáb-pa (ltab pa); *ldáb-ste zér-na* (*ldab ste zer na*) W. saying it once more, again, in short. — 2. ? Ld.: *ldab zúm-te kyer* (*ldab zum te kyer*) take a firm hold of him (or it) with your hand, and carry him (or it) away!
Cs., ldam-pa (ldam pa), very idle, slothful.
Cs.: 'mean, pitiful, sorry, idle'.
Ld. dubious, uncertain, used of things.
Cs. to be weary, tired, faint, languid, ldar-ldár-du gyúr-ba (ldar ldar du gyur ba).
(v. ldir-ba (ldir ba)) the rolling of thunder Thgr.
to fall or sink through Sch.

to be swimming, floating, cf. rkyál-ba (rkyal ba), W.: *čáṅ-ṅi ḱa-tóg-la pabs ldiṅ dug* (*cang ngi kha tog la phabs lding dug*), opp. to *tíl-la ner or nub* (*til la ner or nub*); to be suspended, floating, soaring (in the air), rnám-la (rnam la), nám-mḱa-la (nam mkha' la); mḱá-ldiṅ (mkha' lding) v. mḱa (mkha').
v. ltiṅ-ka (lting ka).
a bower formed by the branches of a tree, the leafy canopy of a dense wood Mil.; śiṅ gyú-lo rgyás-pai ldiṅ-kaṅ (shing g.yu lo rgyas pa'i lding khang) the wide shady porches of turkois-leaved trees.

an officer over fifty, acc. to others, over a hundred men, = brgyá-dpon (brgya dpon), a sergeant, captain, distinguished by a copper button on his cap, Hook. II, 160. 200.; ldiṅ-og (lding 'og) Sch., ldiṅ-tso (lding tsho), the troop under this officer's command.

or ldiṅ-si (lding si) Ld., adv. quite, very, very much, *ṅa ldiṅ-se kams záṅ-po yod* (*nga lding se khams zang po yod*) I am quite well; *ṅa ldiṅ-se ma tạ́d soṅ* (*nga lding se ma thad song*) I was very much displeased, very vexed; perh. also *ldiṅs tág-pa-nas* (*ldings tag pa nas*) for gtiṅ (gting), cf. liṅs-pa (lings pa), or perh. in Ld. ldiṅ (lding) is the form for gtiṅ (gting).
1. vb., pf. bldib (bldib), Sch. = ldig-pa (ldig pa). — 2. adj. Cs.: not clear, not intelligible, *ḱa-dib* (*kha dib*) W. stammering, stuttering; ldib-ldib (ldib ldib) = ldab-ldib (ldab ldib).
W. the crash of a falling tree, the report of a gun, *ldim zér-ra rag* (*ldim zer ra rag*) I hear a crack.
is said to be = *di-ri-ri* (*di ri ri*) C.
